steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Use the perpendicularity property
steps.content:
A tangent line to a circle is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency. The radius goes from the center $(-1, 3)$ to the tangent point $(5, -1)$.

Radius slope: $$m_{\text{radius}} = \frac{-1-3}{5-(-1)} = \frac{-4}{6} = -\frac{2}{3}$$
Since the tangent line is perpendicular, its slope is the negative reciprocal: $$m_{\text{tangent}} = \frac{3}{2}$$
